releases.name: Windows Security Lite v0.1.0
releases.author: auditadmin
releases.body_md:
## AuditToolkit Windows Security Lite — v0.1.0

Authenticode-signed MSI installer for Windows host/server security auditing.

### Artifacts
| File | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| `AuditToolkitWinSecLite-0.1.0.msi` | Authenticode-signed (SHA-256, RFC-3161 timestamp) |
| `audittoolkit-codesign.cer` | Public code-signing certificate (for allow-listing) |
| `SHA256SUMS` | Integrity checksums |

### Signing & trust
Signed by **`CN=AuditToolkit Code Signing, O=AuditToolkitLabs`**
(thumbprint `93708C6494159152678FA51145D15C656F17AD2A`, DigiCert-timestamped).

This is an AuditToolkit-issued publisher certificate intended for **data-centre /
server deployment**. The signature provides tamper-evidence regardless of trust;
where you enforce **WDAC / AppLocker**, allow-list the publisher once using the
included `.cer` (see `docs/WINDOWS-CODE-SIGNING.md`). SmartScreen / S-mode are
consumer-desktop features and do not apply to Windows Server / silent installs.

```powershell
Get-AuthenticodeSignature .\AuditToolkitWinSecLite-0.1.0.msi | Format-List Status, SignerCertificate
# Thumbprint must read 93708C6494159152678FA51145D15C656F17AD2A
```

### Install
```powershell
msiexec /i AuditToolkitWinSecLite-0.1.0.msi /qn /l*v install.log
audittoolkit-windows-security-lite run
```
